openmeter/ingest/httpdriver.purpose: HTTP transport layer for CloudEvent ingestion. Decodes incoming requests across three wire formats (application/json, application/cloudevents+json, application/cloudevents-batch+json) into ingest.IngestEventsRequest and delegates to ingest.Service. Mounted by openmeter/server/router; must stay DI-agnostic.

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandler triple
patterns.description: Every endpoint is constructed with httptransport.NewHandler(decoderFn, operationFn, encoderFn, ...options). Decoder extracts namespace + body; operation calls the service; encoder writes the response.
patterns.example: httptransport.NewHandler(decoder, func(ctx, req) (resp, error) { return h.service.IngestEvents(ctx, req) }, commonhttp.EmptyResponseEncoder[IngestEventsResponse](http.StatusNoContent), opts...)

patterns.name: Handler interface composition
patterns.description: handler.go defines a Handler interface composed of sub-interfaces (IngestHandler). Each method returns a typed httptransport.Handler alias so callers depend on the narrowest surface.
patterns.example: type Handler interface { IngestHandler }; type IngestHandler interface { IngestEvents() IngestEventsHandler }

patterns.name: Namespace resolution via NamespaceDecoder
patterns.description: Namespace is always resolved from context through h.resolveNamespace(ctx) which calls namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder.GetNamespace(ctx). Never read namespace from URL path params.

patterns.name: Domain-local error types + errorEncoder chain
patterns.description: errors.go defines package-local error structs (ErrorInvalidContentType, ErrorInvalidEvent) that implement Error()/Message()/Details(). errorEncoder() maps them to 400 via commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.

patterns.name: Content-type switch for multi-format ingestion
patterns.description: ingest.go switches on Content-Type header to decode application/json (single or batch via AsEvent/AsIngestEventsBody1), application/cloudevents+json, and application/cloudevents-batch+json. Unknown types return ErrorInvalidContentType.
patterns.example: switch contentType { case "application/cloudevents+json": ... case "application/cloudevents-batch+json": ... default: return req, ErrorInvalidContentType{ContentType: contentType} }

key_file_guides.role: Constructs the handler struct and exposes New(). All dependency injection (NamespaceDecoder, ingest.Service, HandlerOptions) happens here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic here — wiring only. Always accept variadic httptransport.HandlerOption so callers can attach middleware.

key_file_guides.role: Implements IngestEvents() endpoint: multi-format request decoding and service dispatch. Type aliases IngestEventsRequest/Response/Handler live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The application/json body can be a single event OR a batch — both paths via AsEvent() and AsIngestEventsBody1() must be tried before returning ErrorInvalidEvent.

key_file_guides.role: Defines domain-local HTTP error types and the errorEncoder() factory passed to httptransport.NewHandler.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Error types must implement Error() and Message(). Register new errors in errorEncoder() via commonhttp.HandleErrorIfTypeMatches — raw errors bypass encoding and produce 500s.

key_file_guides.role: Table-driven tests using httptest.NewServer + ingest.NewInMemoryCollector. Covers single event, batch, and invalid-body paths.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use StaticNamespaceDecoder('test') — always use a deterministic namespace so collector.Events('test') is predictable.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic (validation, transformation) inside the decoder function — delegate to ingest.Service instead
Returning raw errors from the decoder without wrapping in ErrorInvalidEvent or ErrorInvalidContentType — they bypass the errorEncoder and produce 500s
Reading namespace from URL path params instead of h.resolveNamespace(ctx)
Importing app/common or wire providers — this package must stay DI-agnostic
Defining a new endpoint without adding it to the Handler / IngestHandler interface composition in handler.go

decisions.decision: Content-type switch instead of a single decoder
decisions.rationale: CloudEvents spec defines three wire formats (JSON, cloudevents+json, cloudevents-batch+json); dispatching by Content-Type is spec-correct and keeps each parse path isolated.

decisions.decision: Domain-local error types instead of generic HTTP errors
decisions.rationale: ErrorInvalidContentType and ErrorInvalidEvent carry structured details (contentType field) useful for client debugging and are matched by type in errorEncoder to produce precise 400 responses.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new ingest endpoint following the existing pattern

openmeter/ingest/ingestadapter.purpose: OTel instrumentation decorator for ingest.Collector. Wraps any Collector implementation to emit trace spans and two Int64Counters (events ingested, errors) without altering ingest behavior. Applied at wiring time in app/common.

patterns.name: Decorator / wrapper pattern over ingest.Collector
patterns.description: collectorTelemetry embeds an ingest.Collector and implements the same interface. WithTelemetry() is the sole constructor — callers pass in the inner collector and receive an instrumented collector back.
patterns.example: return &collectorTelemetry{collector: collector, tracer: tracer, ingestEventsCounter: c1, ingestErrorsCounter: c2}, nil

patterns.name: Pre-allocated metric instruments in constructor
patterns.description: Counters are created once in WithTelemetry and stored on the struct. Never call metric.Meter.Int64Counter inside Ingest() — instrument registration is not cheap.

patterns.name: Span-per-call with RecordError on failure
patterns.description: Ingest() opens a trace span, delegates to the inner collector, then calls span.RecordError + span.SetStatus on error before incrementing the errors counter. Always defer span.End().
patterns.example: ctx, span := c.tracer.Start(ctx, "openmeter.ingest.events", ...); defer span.End(); err = c.collector.Ingest(...); if err != nil { span.RecordError(err); span.SetStatus(otelcodes.Error, ...) }

patterns.name: Namespace attribute on every metric and span
patterns.description: Both counters and the span carry attribute.String("namespace", namespace) so metrics are filterable per tenant without additional label cardinality.

patterns.name: Metric naming convention openmeter.<domain>.<noun>
patterns.description: Counter names follow openmeter.<domain>.<noun> (e.g. openmeter.ingest.events, openmeter.ingest.errors) with unit strings in UCUM {event}/{error} notation.
patterns.example: metricMeter.Int64Counter("openmeter.ingest.errors", metric.WithDescription("Number of failed event ingests"), metric.WithUnit("{error}"))

key_file_guides.role: Sole file. Defines collectorTelemetry struct, WithTelemetry constructor, and implements Ingest/Close. Metric counters are pre-allocated in the constructor, not per-call.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Close() must delegate to the inner collector. Never swallow the inner collector's error — always propagate after recording the span error.

anti_patterns: Creating metric instruments inside Ingest() instead of in the constructor — causes allocation and registration overhead on every event
Swallowing the inner collector's error — always propagate it after recording the span error
Adding business logic or event transformation here — this package must remain a pure observability decorator
Omitting the namespace attribute from counters — tenant-scoped metrics are required for per-namespace observability

decisions.decision: Separate ingestadapter package instead of embedding telemetry in kafkaingest
decisions.rationale: Keeps observability concerns decoupled from transport — any Collector implementation (kafkaingest, in-memory, test stub) can be wrapped without modification.

code_examples.scenario: Wrapping a collector with telemetry at wiring time
